questions.question: Explain positional-only (`/`) and keyword-only (`*`) parameters in Python 3.8+.
questions.answer: Parameters before `/` can ONLY be passed positionally. Parameters after `*` can ONLY be passed using keyword arguments. E.g. `def f(a, /, b, *, c):` — `a` is positional-only, `b` is either, `c` is keyword-only.
